name: Can I get workers comp for occupational illness if I had a pre-existing condition that work made worse, like in Ogden?
description: Idaho says no for aggravation[1]; prove distinct work disease with expert MD; consult lawyer.

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp for occupational illness if I had a pre-existing condition that work made worse, like in Ogden?

acceptedAnswer.text: Idaho says no for aggravation[1]; prove distinct work disease with expert MD; consult lawyer.
acceptedAnswer.description:
No, generally not in Idaho; aggravation of pre-existing conditions isn't compensable as occupational disease[1].

Idaho law excludes preexisting conditions worsened by work unless it's a true new occupational disease[1]. Utah may allow if work substantially contributed.

- Prove work caused a distinct disease, not just aggravation.
- Medical experts must differentiate.
- Exceptions rare, like Stevens case[1].

Get a second opinion from occupational specialist; attorney review needed to assess viability before filing.
